title: 6. Points for Facial Symmetry on Side of Face
text:
Area on the side of the face
~ !  ~
94
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
~ !  ~
95
⓵ Temporalis
The temporalis is a mastication muscle on
each side of the head that ﬁlls the temporal
fossa,  superior  to  the  zygomatic  arch,  so  it
covers much of the temporal bone. It is also
related to tension headaches.
⓶ Masseter
The masseter is the most powerful muscle of
mastication. It is also the most superﬁcial and
can  become  enlarged  in  patients  who  suffer
from bruxism, or excessive jaw-clenching.
⓷ Lateral Pterygoid
The lateral pterygoid is a mastication muscle
attached  to  the  disk  of  the  TMJ  and  the
sphenoid bone. It pulls the head of the condyle
of the mandible bone out of the TMJ.
⓸ Medial Pterygoid
The medial pterygoid consists of two heads.
The deep head arises from the medial surface of
the lateral pterygoid plate. The superﬁcial head
originates  from  the  maxillary  tuberosity.  It
closes the jaw by the elevation of the mandible
bone.
⓸
⓷
⓶
⓵
MUSCLES ON THE SIDE OF THE FACE
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
• Temporalis *2
• Masseter *2
• Lateral Pterygoid *2
• Medial Pterygoid *2
The masseter group muscles are not facial expression muscles but rather regular skeletal
muscles. They are not attached to the skin, but they connect two bones. They connect the
skull and the jaw bone, so they hold and close the jaw with incredible strength. It can exert
the greatest amount of pressure among all the muscles according to the book, Clinical Oral
Science  - “A small but strong fellow.”
18
If you have a biting problem, like the habit of chewing food only with one side, a dental
problem like gum disease, a TMJ disorder, or a teeth grinding issue, there is a good chance
these two jaw muscles are asymmetric. First, these problems make the size of the jaw muscles
on left and right different. The size difference could be one common cosmetic issue. Second,
if one side of the masseter is stronger than the other, and it is constantly in tension, the jaw
would be tilted to that side. If the left masseter is shortened and tightened, the jaw would be
elevated upward on left side like the picture below. Like domino falling one by one, it affects
the symmetry of other muscles’. Eventually, it can affect the neck and even the pelvis.
The lateral pterygoid pulls the jaw forward, so if it is tightened, the jaw is constantly
pulled forward, and can be dislocated easily.
In cosmetic acupuncture, you should be able to release
those two muscles well. Needling ST-6 and progressing the
needle forward about 3-4cm can reach the lateral pterygoid.
For the masseter, you put needles on trigger points around
ST-7 and release it with a massage. Also, the patient should
be instructed to correct their old, wrong habits related to
symmetry of face like a biting habit, grinding of the teeth,
habitual  clenching,  chewing  gums,  chewing  hard
substances, etc.
Michael Edgar et al. Clinical Oral Science, 1e. Butterworth-Heinemann, 1998.
